---
title: "Week 9: Mar 23 - Mar 29, 2026"
subtitle: "Unit III: JSP Advanced & MVC Architecture"
---

## Week Overview

This week covers advanced JSP features, JSTL, MVC architecture, and database connectivity with JDBC.

**Course Outcome:** CO-3, CO-4 (K3-K4 - Application & Analysis)  
**Duration:** 1 week (4 lectures + 1 lab)  
**Lab:** JSP & Database Integration

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Quick Practice Quizzes (Lectures 25-28)
Use the [Quiz Hub](/quiz/index.qmd) or jump directly to:

- [Lecture 25 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-25-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 26 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-26-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 27 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-27-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 28 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-28-quiz.qmd)
:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
This week uses upcoming lecture decks. Use the [Lectures Index](/lectures/index.qmd) for all available slides.
:::

---

## Lecture 25: Handling HTTP GET & POST Requests {#lecture-25}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-25-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-25.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/index.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### JSP Directives
```jsp
<!-- Page directive -->
<%@ page language="java" contentType="text/html; charset=UTF-8" 
         import="java.util.*" errorPage="error.jsp" %>

<!-- Include directive -->
<%@ include file="header.jsp" %>

<!-- Taglib directive -->
<%@ taglib prefix="c" uri="http://java.sun.com/jsp/jstl/core" %>
```

### Implicit Objects
```jsp
<!-- request: HttpServletRequest -->
<%= request.getParameter("name") %>
<%= request.getRequestURI() %>

<!-- response: HttpServletResponse -->
<% response.sendRedirect("newpage.jsp"); %>

<!-- session: HttpSession -->
<% session.setAttribute("userId", "123"); %>

<!-- out: PrintWriter (response output) -->
<% out.println("Output text"); %>

<!-- application: ServletContext - shared data -->
<% application.setAttribute("appName", "MyApp"); %>

<!-- config: ServletConfig -->
<%= config.getServletName() %>

<!-- pageContext: PageContext - all JSP objects -->
<% pageContext.setAttribute("var", "value"); %>

<!-- exception: Throwable (in error pages) -->
<%= exception.getMessage() %>

<!-- page: Object (reference to current page) -->
```

---

## Lecture 26: Redirecting Requests & Request Dispatching {#lecture-26}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-26-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-26.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-26.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Redirect vs Forward (Simple View)

- **Redirect**: browser gets new URL and sends new request
- **Forward**: server internally sends same request to another resource
- Redirect changes URL; forward usually keeps same URL
- Redirect loses request attributes; forward keeps them

### Servlet Example
```java
if (isValidUser) {
  response.sendRedirect("home.jsp");
} else {
  request.setAttribute("error", "Invalid login");
  request.getRequestDispatcher("login.jsp").forward(request, response);
}
```

### When to Use Which

- Use redirect after successful form submission (PRG pattern)
- Use forward to show validation errors on same flow
- Use forward when JSP needs request attributes

---

## Lecture 27: Cookies & Session Tracking {#lecture-27}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-27-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-27.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-27.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Why Session Tracking is Needed

- HTTP is stateless, so server does not remember old requests by default
- We need tracking for login state, cart, and personalized pages

### Cookie Example
```java
Cookie c = new Cookie("user", "rahul");
c.setMaxAge(3600);
response.addCookie(c);
```

### HttpSession Example
```java
HttpSession session = request.getSession();
session.setAttribute("loggedInUser", "rahul@gcet.ac.in");
```

### Key Difference

- Cookies are stored in browser (small data)
- Session data is stored on server (safer for login state)

---

## Lecture 28: Session Tracking using HttpSession {#lecture-28}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-28-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-28.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/index.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### JDBC Connection
```java
import java.sql.*;

// Load driver
Class.forName("com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver");

// Create connection
String url = "jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/mydb";
String user = "root";
String password = "password";
Connection conn = DriverManager.getConnection(url, user, password);
```

### Execute Query
```java
// PreparedStatement (secure against SQL injection)
String query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?";
PreparedStatement pstmt = conn.prepareStatement(query);
pstmt.setInt(1, userId);

ResultSet rs = pstmt.executeQuery();
while (rs.next()) {
  String name = rs.getString("name");
  String email = rs.getString("email");
}

rs.close();
pstmt.close();
conn.close();
```

### Insert/Update/Delete
```java
String insertSQL = "INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES (?, ?)";
PreparedStatement pstmt = conn.prepareStatement(insertSQL);
pstmt.setString(1, "John");
pstmt.setString(2, "john@example.com");
int rows = pstmt.executeUpdate();  // Returns affected rows
```

---

## Lab 7: JSP Pages & Database Integration

### Objectives
- Build JSP applications with database
- Implement MVC pattern
- Use JSTL for templates
- JDBC for data access

### Activities
1. Create user registration form
2. Store data in database
3. Display user list from database
4. Update/delete user functionality
5. Implement basic MVC structure

### Deliverables
- JSP application with CRUD operations
- Database integration
- Working MVC implementation

---

## Key Takeaways

✅ JSP implicit objects provide access to request/response  
✅ JSTL reduces need for Java code in JSP  
✅ MVC separates concerns (controller, model, view)  
✅ JDBC provides Java API for database access  
✅ PreparedStatement prevents SQL injection

---

## Next Week

Unit IV begins:
- Spring Framework fundamentals
- Dependency Injection
- Spring Beans and application context
